dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 28, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**RAYS SHUT DOWN GUARDIANS IN 1-0 WIN**

In a low-scoring affair, the Tampa Bay Rays edged the Cleveland Guardians 1-0, behind a strong pitching performance from Nick Martinez and Cole Sulser. Jonathan Aranda's single in the top of the 2nd inning proved to be the difference-maker, scoring Taylor Walls and giving the Rays the lead. On the mound, Tanner Bibee was solid for the Guardians, but ultimately took the loss.

**ORIOLES RALLY PAST ASTROS IN 5-3 WIN**

The Baltimore Orioles continued their hot streak, defeating the Houston Astros 5-3 behind a dominant outing from Shane Baz. Brice Matthews was a key contributor, hitting a two-run homer in the 3rd inning to give the Orioles a 3-1 lead. Matthews finished the game with three hits and two RBIs.

**REDS SMASH ROCKIES IN 7-2 WIN**

The Cincinnati Reds exploded for seven runs in the first three innings, cruising to a 7-2 win over the Colorado Rockies. Elly De La Cruz was a standout performer, hitting a three-run homer in the 2nd inning to give the Reds a 4-0 lead. Chase Burns was impressive on the mound, striking out nine in six innings of work.

**CARDINALS RALLY PAST PIRATES IN 11-7 WIN**

The St. Louis Cardinals overcame an early 4-0 deficit to defeat the Pittsburgh Pirates 11-7. Ryan O'Hearn was a key contributor, hitting a two-run homer in the 5th inning to give the Cardinals a 7-5 lead. Kyle Leahy was solid on the mound, striking out seven in five and a third innings.

**PHILLIES SHUT DOWN GIANTS IN 7-0 WIN**

The Philadelphia Phillies dominated the San Francisco Giants 7-0, behind a strong outing from Jesús Luzardo. Trea Turner was a standout performer, hitting four singles in five at-bats. Luzardo was impressive on the mound, striking out eight in seven innings of work.

**BLUE JAYS SHUT DOWN RED SOX IN 3-0 WIN**

The Toronto Blue Jays shut down the Boston Red Sox 3-0, behind a strong pitching performance from Louis Varland and Trey Yesavage. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. was a key contributor, hitting a single in the 1st inning to give the Blue Jays a 1-0 lead.

**METS BLITZ NATIONALS IN 8-0 WIN**

The New York Mets dominated the Washington Nationals 8-0, behind a strong outing from Clay Holmes. Bo Bichette was a standout performer, hitting a solo homer in the 5th inning to give the Mets a 2-0 lead. Holmes was impressive on the mound, striking out six in six innings of work.

**BRAVES EDGE TIGERS IN 5-2 WIN**

The Atlanta Braves edged the Detroit Tigers 5-2, behind a strong outing from Martín Pérez. Wenceel Pérez was a key contributor, hitting a two-run homer in the 3rd inning to give the Braves a 2-0 lead.

**MARINERS BLITZ TWINS IN 7-1 WIN**

The Seattle Mariners dominated the Minnesota Twins 7-1, behind a strong outing from Eduard Bazardo. Josh Naylor was a standout performer, hitting a three-run homer in the 3rd inning to give the Mariners a 3-0 lead.
